title: How Can We Reduce Swelling？ | Dr Tim's Tips
filename: How Can We Reduce Swelling？ ｜ Dr Tim_s Tips.en.vtt
excerpt: So how can we reduce swelling after dermal filler procedures before we even ask this question we need to understand what swelling is so inflammation after procedure it's the body's response to some sort of threat so infection allergy and trauma or any injury also results in infla…

segments.text: so how can we reduce swelling after dermal filler procedures before we even ask this question we need to understand what swelling is so inflammation after procedure it's the body's response to some sort of threat so infection allergy and trauma or any injury also results in inflammation it's your body sending white blood cells to the area the release of cytokines that cause the dilation of blood vessels and increased blood flow to start the healing process so not always is information a bad thing but sometimes it's excessive and we need to control it and of course we don't want this for our clients anyway so there are five things you can do to reduce swelling the first starts with the procedure itself is the number one cause of excessive inflammation and this will boils down to your technique if you're using a technique where you're using excessive numbers of entry points into the skin for example I've seen some versions of the lip tenting procedure where they're entering twenty five times on either

side of the lip you are gonna get much more swelling from each time that needle slides through the skin slicing through individual cells they empty their contents which are basically saying I act as cell mediators pulling the inflammatory response from the rest of the body now so in techniques that are more traumatic or less traumatic involved things like excessive needle use on one end of the scale versus on the lower end of the scale using a cannula where you've only got one entry point and you're very gentle as you slide the instrument through that's how your procedure can affect the result next you've got the product there is a theory that shorter chain hyaluronic acids are pro-inflammatory and longer chain molecules decrease inflammation so sometimes you'll notice more information with your shorter chain dermal fillers than you will with the longer chain products so it may be appropriate to vary the product depending on the patient's response next you must consider the product placement you tend to get a lot more information for example around the lips then you will when treating the cheek or sometimes the temple or the forehead some areas are more prone to inflammation than others

and it's partly due to their vasculature and how much blood flow is in the area already the person is probably the most variable component of all of this some people can have a big procedure and have almost no swelling while other will inflame even on after a small process and this is down to the individual they may have extra information or react slightly to the product that you wouldn't call a an allergic reaction but it's something there that's causing them to respond a little bit more than average I see more difference between individual people and information than I do be to in the same person on different parts of their face there's a big spread from almost none to very significant swelling and it's mainly down to the person and their individual biochemistry finally there are all the things that we normally focus on in terms of decreasing information which are the post procedural practices so we have got the five please the procedure the product the placement the person and the post procedure practice post procedure practices are down to the patient avoiding things that increase blood flow to their skin so if you immediately do exercise after procedure you will get

all more inflammation similarly if you immediately were to go in a sunbed or get extra sun or get warm for any reason that's also going to increase information we also have an ability to decrease inflammation at this stage by practices that decrease blood flow so applying ice might decrease blood flow to the skin while similarly if you treat with non-steroidal inflammatory inflammatories antihistamines or in rare cases steroids all of those will decrease the inflammatory response which means you have less swelling after the procedure and that's how you decrease inflammation and swelling from dermal fillers
